title: Expressing opposition to Central Business District Tolling Program of New York City.
summary:
OVERVIEW: This bill expresses Congress’s disapproval of a plan by New York City to charge drivers a fee to enter parts of Manhattan. It aims to halt the implementation of this congestion pricing program.

WHO IS AFFECTED:
- Drivers who travel into parts of Manhattan.
- Residents and businesses in/around the tolled area. 

WHAT CHANGES:
- Could block New York City’s congestion pricing plan.
- May require federal funds be redirected away from the project.

WHAT'S DEBATED:
- Supporters say: The program unfairly burdens commuters and hinders access to Manhattan.
- Critics say: It interferes with local decisions and a revenue source for mass transit. 

STATUS: This bill has been introduced in the House of Representatives and is currently awaiting consideration by relevant committees.
status: Referred to the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure.
